background.exact_age_and_generation: 28 years old, Zillennial (late Millennial/early Gen Z)
background.occupation: Junior Data Analyst at a large insurance corporation
background.social_class: Working Class / Lower Income (lives paycheck-to-paycheck in a shared apartment despite white-collar job)
background.key_experience: Got laid off from his first job out of college during the pandemic when his company replaced his entire team with an AI automation system - now sees all tech advancements as direct threats to job security

dominant_traits: Very High Neuroticism (constantly anxious about job stability)
Very Low Openness to Experience (resists new technologies)

cognition.core_value: Job security above all else - believes stable employment is the foundation of a decent life
cognition.bias: Negativity bias - immediately assumes any new technology or workplace change will lead to layoffs, automation replacing humans, or reduced hours

current_state.recent_memory: His manager mentioned 'exploring AI tools to optimize workflows' in last week's team meeting
current_state.emotion: Anxious and resentful - convinced his company is about to automate his position despite no concrete evidence

online_behavior.typical_posts: Comments on tech news articles with doom scenarios about AI taking jobs, shares viral posts about automation dangers without fact-checking, joins anti-automation groups but rarely starts discussions
online_behavior.influence_patterns: Easily swayed by viral 'AI will destroy all jobs' content, changes opinions based on the last alarming article he read, uses corporate jargon he hears at work to sound authoritative
online_behavior.argument_style: Uses personal anxiety as evidence ('This WILL happen because I'm scared it will'), cites viral social media posts as sources, quickly adopts whatever anti-tech position is trending in his feeds
